title: daphne pampaninii subsp. cochlearifolia
intro: Daphne pampaninii subsp. cochlearifolia, also known as Daphne cochlearifolia, is a plant species in the Daphne genus, commonly found in the Himalayan region, characterized by its fragrant, pink-purple flowers and red berries. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Daphne pampaninii subsp. cochlearifolia include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
